Assignment - Week 03

Yara Roth

2026-03-09

Code

Setup

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(AnnotationHub)
  library(Rsubread)
  library(rtracklayer)
  library(Biostrings)
  library(Rfastp)
  library(epiwraps)
})
ah <- AnnotationHub()

library(rstudioapi)
setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))

Download ChiP-seq for CTCF protein

dir.create("data")
## Warning in dir.create("data"): 'data' already exists
dir.create("data/raw")
## Warning in dir.create("data/raw"): 'data/raw' already exists
if (!file.exists("data/raw/ctcf_input.fastq.gz")) {
  download.file("https://www.encodeproject.org/files/ENCFF127RRR/@@download/ENCFF127RRR.fastq.gz", "data/raw/ctcf_input.fastq.gz", timeout = 5000)
} else {
  print("File already exists.")
}
## [1] "File already exists."

Quality Control and Trimming

Quality Control

Now we will perform quality control, to look at the quality of the reads. I did perform this directly in my terminal.

mkdir -p data/raw_fastqc
fastqc -o data/raw_fastqc --threads 4 data/raw/*.fastq.gz

The html file with the results can be found in week03/data/raw_fastqc/.
The quality of the reads is not optimal, we have a mean quality score of ~23, with increasing variability towards the end of the reads. No adapter sequences were identified.

Trimming

dir.create("data/rfastp.trimmed")
## Warning in dir.create("data/rfastp.trimmed"): 'data/rfastp.trimmed' already
## exists
qc <- Rfastp::rfastp(
  read1="data/raw/ctcf_input.fastq.gz",
  thread=4,
  overrepresentationAnalysis=TRUE,
  adapterTrimming = TRUE,
  outputFastq="data/rfastp.trimmed/ctcf_trimmed"
)

Reads get trimmed if mean quality drops below 20 for a bp window of 4 or if an adapter gets detected. 98.26% passed the filtering, meaning they had sufficient quality (less than 40% bases below Q15) and had a goon N content.

Rfastp::curvePlot(qc, curve="content_curves")
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the Rfastp package.
##   Please report the issue to the authors.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Rfastp::curvePlot(qc, curve="quality_curves")

Alignment

Now we want to get the genome sequence for the drosophila melangaster in a TwoBit format for BDGP6 (we already downloaded it last week). We will use the latest available genome.

Retrieving drosophila genome

query_results <- query(ah, c("Drosophila melanogaster","TwoBitFile","dna_sm","BDGP6"))

mcols(query_results)[, c("title", "rdatadateadded")]
## DataFrame with 24 rows and 2 columns
##                           title rdatadateadded
##                     <character>    <character>
## AH49674  Drosophila_melanogas..     2015-12-28
## AH50019  Drosophila_melanogas..     2015-12-29
## AH50510  Drosophila_melanogas..     2016-05-03
## AH51198  Drosophila_melanogas..     2016-08-15
## AH51544  Drosophila_melanogas..     2016-11-03
## ...                         ...            ...
## AH90691  Drosophila_melanogas..     2020-10-27
## AH92960  Drosophila_melanogas..     2021-05-17
## AH99424  Drosophila_melanogas..     2021-10-20
## AH103729 Drosophila_melanogas..     2022-04-21
## AH106160 Drosophila_melanogas..     2022-04-25
query_results[length(query_results)]
## AnnotationHub with 1 record
## # snapshotDate(): 2025-10-29
## # names(): AH106160
## # $dataprovider: Ensembl
## # $species: drosophila melanogaster
## # $rdataclass: TwoBitFile
## # $rdatadateadded: 2022-04-25
## # $title: Drosophila_melanogaster.BDGP6.32.dna_sm.toplevel.2bit
## # $description: TwoBit DNA sequence for drosophila melanogaster
## # $taxonomyid: 7227
## # $genome: BDGP6.32
## # $sourcetype: FASTA
## # $sourceurl: ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-107/fasta/drosophila_melanog...
## # $sourcesize: NA
## # $tags: c("TwoBit", "ensembl", "sequence", "2bit", "FASTA") 
## # retrieve record with 'object[["AH106160"]]'
genome_d <- ah[["AH106160"]]
## loading from cache
dir.create("data/BDGP6_genome")
## Warning in dir.create("data/BDGP6_genome"): 'data/BDGP6_genome' already exists
export(import.2bit(genome_d), "data/BDGP6_genome/genome.fasta.gz", compress=TRUE)

Building Genome Index

Now we will build the genome index based on our BDGP6 genome, which will allow us to map/align the reads.

if (!file.exists("data/BDGP6_genome/rsubread.files")) {
  Rsubread::buildindex("data/BDGP6_genome/rsubread", reference="data/BDGP6_genome/genome.fasta.gz")
} else {
  message("Index already built.")
}
## Index already built.

Alignment

Now we will perform the mapping.

dir.create("data/aligned")
## Warning in dir.create("data/aligned"): 'data/aligned' already exists
align.stats <- Rsubread::align(
  index="data/BDGP6_genome/rsubread",
  type="dna",
  readfile1="data/rfastp.trimmed/ctcf_trimmed_R1.fastq.gz",output_file="data/aligned/ctcf.bam",
  nthreads=6,
  sortReadsByCoordinates=TRUE
)
## 
##         ==========     _____ _    _ ____  _____  ______          _____  
##         =====         / ____| |  | |  _ \|  __ \|  ____|   /\   |  __ \ 
##           =====      | (___ | |  | | |_) | |__) | |__     /  \  | |  | |
##             ====      \___ \| |  | |  _ <|  _  /|  __|   / /\ \ | |  | |
##               ====    ____) | |__| | |_) | | \ \| |____ / ____ \| |__| |
##         ==========   |_____/ \____/|____/|_|  \_\______/_/    \_\_____/

##

align.stats
##                       ctcf.bam
## Total_reads             139375
## Mapped_reads            129540
## Uniquely_mapped_reads   112547
## Multi_mapping_reads      16993
## Unmapped_reads            9835
## Indels                      61

We have a mappability of 91.8% which is very high. The bam file can be found in data/aligned/ctcf.bam.

Peak calling

BiocManager::install("MACSr")
## Bioconductor version 3.22 (BiocManager 1.30.27), R 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)
## Warning: package(s) not installed when version(s) same as or greater than current; use
##   `force = TRUE` to re-install: 'MACSr'
library(MACSr)

Now we will extract the peaks and their supplementary information (location) using:

dir.create("data/peaks")
## Warning in dir.create("data/peaks"): 'data/peaks' already exists
callpeak(
  tfile="data/aligned/ctcf.bam",
  gsize="dm",
  outdir = "data/peaks",
  name = "ctcf"
)
## INFO  @ 12 Mar 2026 00:14:34: [2222 MB] 
## # Command line: 
## # ARGUMENTS LIST:
## # name = ctcf
## # format = AUTO
## # ChIP-seq file = ['/Users/yararoth/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-ETHZurich/fs26/ethEpigen2026_material/week03/data/aligned/ctcf.bam']
## # control file = None
## # effective genome size = 1.43e+08
## # band width = 300
## # model fold = [5.0, 50.0]
## # qvalue cutoff = 5.00e-02
## # The maximum gap between significant sites is assigned as the read length/tag size.
## # The minimum length of peaks is assigned as the predicted fragment length "d".
## # Larger dataset will be scaled towards smaller dataset.
## # Range for calculating regional lambda is: 10000 bps
## # Broad region calling is off
## # Additional cutoff on fold-enrichment is: 0.10
## # Paired-End mode is off

peaks <- rtracklayer::import("data/peaks/ctcf_peaks.narrowPeak", format="narrowPeak")
peaks

Report

Mappability

3’505’377 out of total 3’816’999 reads were mapped, which corresponds to a mappability of 91.8%.

Number of identified peaks

A total of 5405 peaks were identified for the protein CTCF.

Peak Signal inside a gene

To identify peaks that lie within a gene, we first need to extract the genes from the drosophila EnsDb.

query_results <- query(ah, c("Drosophila","EnsDb","BDGP6"))
query_results[length(query_results)]
## AnnotationHub with 1 record
## # snapshotDate(): 2025-10-29
## # names(): AH119285
## # $dataprovider: Ensembl
## # $species: Drosophila melanogaster
## # $rdataclass: EnsDb
## # $rdatadateadded: 2024-10-28
## # $title: Ensembl 113 EnsDb for Drosophila melanogaster
## # $description: Gene and protein annotations for Drosophila melanogaster bas...
## # $taxonomyid: 7227
## # $genome: BDGP6.46
## # $sourcetype: ensembl
## # $sourceurl: http://www.ensembl.org
## # $sourcesize: NA
## # $tags: c("113", "Annotation", "AnnotationHubSoftware", "Coverage",
## #   "DataImport", "EnsDb", "Ensembl", "Gene", "Protein", "Sequencing",
## #   "Transcript") 
## # retrieve record with 'object[["AH119285"]]'
ensdb_d <- ah[["AH119285"]]
## loading from cache
## require("ensembldb")
genes_d <- genes(ensdb_d)
head(genes_d)
## GRanges object with 6 ranges and 9 metadata columns:
##                                       seqnames      ranges strand |
##                                          <Rle>   <IRanges>  <Rle> |
##                    FBgn0267594 211000022278279 11634-12709      - |
##                    FBgn0259849 211000022278436   1551-2815      - |
##                    FBgn0085494 211000022278449    528-1635      - |
##                    FBgn0085506 211000022278760     562-879      - |
##   RR41606_transposable_element 211000022278978    515-2242      + |
##   RR51478_transposable_element 211000022278978   2243-3964      + |
##                                               gene_id       gene_name
##                                           <character>     <character>
##                    FBgn0267594            FBgn0267594         CR45932
##                    FBgn0259849            FBgn0259849 Su(Ste):CR42418
##                    FBgn0085494            FBgn0085494    Mst77Y-16Psi
##                    FBgn0085506            FBgn0085506         CG40635
##   RR41606_transposable_element RR41606_transposable..  Bari1{}RR41606
##   RR51478_transposable_element RR51478_transposable..  Bari1{}RR51478
##                                        gene_biotype seq_coord_system
##                                         <character>      <character>
##                    FBgn0267594           pseudogene primary_assembly
##                    FBgn0259849                ncRNA primary_assembly
##                    FBgn0085494           pseudogene primary_assembly
##                    FBgn0085506       protein_coding primary_assembly
##   RR41606_transposable_element transposable_element primary_assembly
##   RR51478_transposable_element transposable_element primary_assembly
##                                           description        gene_id_version
##                                           <character>            <character>
##                    FBgn0267594                   NULL            FBgn0267594
##                    FBgn0259849        Su(Ste):CR42418            FBgn0259849
##                    FBgn0085494 Male-specific pseudo..            FBgn0085494
##                    FBgn0085506                   NULL            FBgn0085506
##   RR41606_transposable_element                   NULL RR41606_transposable..
##   RR51478_transposable_element                   NULL RR51478_transposable..
##                                  canonical_transcript          symbol entrezid
##                                           <character>     <character>   <list>
##                    FBgn0267594            FBtr0347033         CR45932     <NA>
##                    FBgn0259849            FBtr0300146 Su(Ste):CR42418  7354452
##                    FBgn0085494            FBtr0308947    Mst77Y-16Psi  5740181
##                    FBgn0085506            FBtr0302344         CG40635  5740827
##   RR41606_transposable_element RR41606_transposable..  Bari1{}RR41606     <NA>
##   RR51478_transposable_element RR51478_transposable..  Bari1{}RR51478     <NA>
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 29 sequences (1 circular) from BDGP6.46 genome

When I tried to extract the peaks that lie within a gene, I got a warning that I have a mismatch between the naming conventions of peaks and genes_d. I tried to solve it with the following chunk. Unfortunately, I still received a warning after and did not manage to resolve the issue completely. Maybe we could look at that in the lecture.

seqlevelsStyle(peaks) <- seqlevelsStyle(genes_d)
## Warning in .normarg_seqlevelsStyle(value): more than one seqlevels style
## supplied, using the 1st one only
peaks_in_genes <- suppressWarnings(peaks[overlapsAny(peaks, genes_d, type="within")])
peaks_in_genes
## GRanges object with 403 ranges and 6 metadata columns:
##                     seqnames        ranges strand |           name     score
##                        <Rle>     <IRanges>  <Rle> |    <character> <numeric>
##     [1]                   2L 490100-490441      * |   ctcf_peak_80       762
##     [2]                   2L 492353-492583      * |   ctcf_peak_81       115
##     [3]                   2L 740054-740411      * |   ctcf_peak_83       473
##     [4]                   2L 855341-855522      * |   ctcf_peak_84        63
##     [5]                   2L 871591-871931      * |   ctcf_peak_85       279
##     ...                  ...           ...    ... .            ...       ...
##   [399] mitochondrion_genome     8581-8807      * | ctcf_peak_1072        66
##   [400] mitochondrion_genome   10566-11086      * | ctcf_peak_1073        90
##   [401] mitochondrion_genome   12790-12969      * | ctcf_peak_1074        87
##   [402]                 rDNA   36296-36474      * | ctcf_peak_1075        42
##   [403]                 rDNA   44371-44592      * | ctcf_peak_1076        69
##         signalValue    pValue    qValue      peak
##           <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <integer>
##     [1]    23.00250  81.05440  76.24440       169
##     [2]     7.06895  14.78230  11.59590       119
##     [3]    20.38110  51.30650  47.32460       204
##     [4]     6.45717   9.35592   6.34264        90
##     [5]    14.91660  31.44640  27.92060       199
##     ...         ...       ...       ...       ...
##   [399]     3.37459   9.71531   6.68888        80
##   [400]     4.38590  12.18510   9.07130       416
##   [401]     4.73045  11.83630   8.73124        89
##   [402]     5.22349   7.11011   4.21253        52
##   [403]     5.29630   9.95063   6.91371        71
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 85 sequences from an unspecified genome; no seqlengths

Now i will select the biggest peak we have (so the one with the highest score).

biggest_peak_idx <- which.max(peaks_in_genes$score)
biggest_peak <- peaks_in_genes[biggest_peak_idx]

Next I’ll extraxt the name of the gene, in which the peak is located.

gene_idx <- suppressWarnings(subjectHits(findOverlaps(biggest_peak, genes_d)))
gene_name <- genes_d[gene_idx]$gene_name
gene_name
## [1] "R1A1{}1124"

And lastly we can plot everything:

plotSignalTracks(
  list(ctcf = "data/aligned/ctcf.bam"),
  region = biggest_peak,
  extend = 500,
  genomeAxis = TRUE,
  ensdb = ensdb_d,
  transcripts = "full",
  main = paste("CTCF peaks inside the", gene_name, "gene")
)
## Warning in getUTRsByTranscript(x = x, what = "five", columns = columns, : No
## fiveUTR found!
## Warning in getUTRsByTranscript(x = x, what = "three", columns = columns, : No
## threeUTR found!
## Warning in .local(x, by, ...): No cds found!
